pages.text: good evening West Orange Welcome to our conference meeting agenda it is Wednesday October 26 2016 Madame clerk this is to inform the general public that this meeting is being held in compliance with Section 5 of the open public meetings act chapter 231 Public Law 1975 a notice of this meeting was emailed to The Star Ledger in the West Orange Chronicle on October 14th 2015 a notice of this meeting was also posted on the bulletin board in the municipal building West Orange and filed in the office of the municipal clerk of the township of West Orange on October 14th 2015 councilwoman castelino present councilman Gino present councilman kovak present councilwoman mcartney pres council president cillo president thank you very much Madam clerk we're going to get started with Relay for Life is that time of the year again and we have our friends here in the audience with a presentation on what to expect thank you so much for having us and it is earlier in the year than normal but we're get

ting an early start with Relay for Life this year I want to thank you for the opportunity to speak tonight and also for participation last year we had a lot of you come to to relay and whether it was the event or kickoff in Prior years we're just so grateful for for all of your support I want to start by sharing a little bit about why we relay and what it is that the American Cancer Society does with the money and how that impacts us locally one of the biggest things that the American Cancer Society does is something called the National Cancer information center that's a 247 800 number that's available to people who have any questions related to cancer so if you need to speak to someone about local programs and services if you just spoke to your doctor and need help translating the medical language they are there for you 24/7 always in English and Spanish and they can get translators in literally every language they use a language service so that this program is available to every person 247 365 they also help people get matched t

o clinical trials which as a lay person if you need to do that it is a near impossible task but they have a department that's there for that they have an entire department that's dedicated to helping people work through their insurance issues it's complicated and it seems to get more so every single year but they have people that will explain it to you so you know what you're responsible for and you can get the best treatment that you can get another program that the American Cancer Society offers is called road to recovery this is a program that takes people to treatment through a network of volunteers and these are people who would otherwise have no way of getting to their treatment as as I hear locally a lot of people talking about aging in place and and things like that this is a service that allows people to be taken to and from their treatment without having to rely on expensive taxi cabs and things like that so we're able to get people on time to the the treatment that they need without having people need to take off 87 00:03:48,

of work or things like that that's something that we provide very locally another thing another program that's offered is called reach to recovery this pairs women of similar experiences through breast cancer who've been out for 5 years or more in remission with someone who's newly diagnosed so they're able to talk to them about their experience and really be there for them I've talked to women who've gone through this program and the mentors who were there they answer the phone at 300 in the morning they go to their homes they they are real advocates for them and we're able to train these volunteers on how to handle these things appropriately a a another program that the American caner offers and actually we have it right here at St Barnabas nearby it's called look good feel better this is a program that affects the physical aspects of cancer going through chemotherapy and radiation has an extraordinary effect on your hair on your skin and women who attend this program get kits with makeup and

56,360 they show them how to use it in a way that's going to make them feel better we know if you feel better as you go through treatment that's going to have a impact on your morale and everything else we also have a free wigs program where if somebody needs a wig at no charge the American can side will give them a wig and that's here and then one of the the biggest programs that the American Cancer study offers is something called Hope Lodge there are 32 hope lodges across the country and they're based in cities that have extraordinary Cancer Care New York City Boston Houston where there are really great Cancer Centers because if you need to travel to one of those cities for treatment could you imagine what the hotel costs would be so through Hope Lodge both the person receiving treatment and their caregiver can stay at zero charge as long as they need to the American Cancer Society also has a program through Marriott where people can stay at hotels if they need to travel for treatment there are 10,000 nights available for free and 10,000 143

nights available at just $18 a night I think locally if someone needed to travel to New York City what the cost would be for them to stay in New York City while they're going through treatment for a week or two or more and through Relay for Life we're able to pay for that only through Relay for Life are we able to provide such valuable programs and services not just Nationwide but specifically here in West Orange so Relay for Life is a way that we do that and as a quick refresher Relay for Life is an overnight team fundraising event Where We Gather groups of people usually 8 to 8 to 15 and the goal is that at least one person is walking around the track throughout the entire event those are sort of the logistics of it but the really important part of it is that we honor our survivors we remember those we've lost and we fight back against cancer so as I said earlier this is a lot earlier in the year than we'd normally start talking but our committee is already underway with the planning and that's the ask here tonight we have a real n

eed to grow our event leadership team these are the volunteers that put on the event they work year round and they do everything that needs to happen to have that wonderful day in May so if I can I'd like to pass out these are our post postcards save the dates for an interest meeting and what we're really looking for are referrals for people you know that have an interest in fighting cancer and are looking to volunteer we'd obviously love for all of you to join us as well if you can but really this is an opportunity for people to spend a lot of time as you can see I dragged my wife here so it it affects a lot of our time and and she has to come around or you can spend a little bit of time and we're we're really working to grow we we've got a few new interested people this year but really we need a lot of help if we want to keep this event growing in West Orange we need a lot of people to join us and there's no act too small when it comes to volunteerism so we would really appreciate any referrals have and
